content:
Character: Dr. Thaddeus Pelagios
Perception: Thaddeus is a rumpled, mustached, spectacled whirlwind who shows up at 7:45 every morning like clockwork, orders the same thing, and tells me something astonishing about the ocean before I've finished pouring. He's eccentric but never unkind — his tips alone could cover my textbooks. He's genuinely brilliant; I've read one of his papers and it was better argued than half my law professors' articles. I think he sees me as a surrogate niece. I see him as the building's beating heart — proof that passion doesn't have to make you hard. I know about the megalodon obsession, the divorce, the shark named Rosalind.
summary:
Character: Dr. Thaddeus Pelagios
Perception: Thaddeus is a rumpled, mustached, spectacled whirlwind who shows up at 7:45 every morning like clockwork, orders the same thing, and tells me something astonishing about the ocean before I've finished pouring. He's eccentric but never unkind — his tips alone could cover my textbooks. He's genuinely brilliant; I've read one of his papers and it was better argued than half my law professors' articles. I think he sees me as a surrogate niece. I see him as the building's beating heart — proof that passion doesn't have to make you hard. I know about the megalodon obsession, the divorce, the shark named Rosalind.
